metadata.title: Stable Reference IDs Survive Viewport Chaos.

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. You tell Claude to click a button at pixel 960, 540. User resizes the browser. Now the button is at 720, 405. Same button. Different pixels. How do you make the automation survive?

props.segment: Stable Reference IDs Survive Viewport Chaos.
props.command: Assign stable refs to every clickable element on this page so my automation survives a resize.

beats.narration_text: Pixel-based automation ties a command to a location in a specific viewport. Resize the window — the page reflows. The button moves. The coordinates are wrong. The automation breaks. Every time.

props.segment: Stable Reference IDs Survive Viewport Chaos.
props.command: Button 'Confirm Order' at (960, 540) on 1920×1080. Resize to 1440×900. Now at (720, 405). Click missed.

viz.description: Animated browser frame morphing from 1920×1080 to 1440×900. A 'Confirm Order' button is shown at two positions — terracotta dot at (960,540) labeled '1920×1080', then the frame shrinks and the dot moves to (720,405) labeled '1440×900'. A red X appears over the first coordinate pair as it becomes invalid. INK labels throughout. Source: Claude Quickstarts (Anthropic).

beats.narration_text: The fix: use JavaScript to assign a stable reference ID to every clickable element before Claude ever sees the page. The ref persists across reflows. Claude targets by ref, not by pixel. The button moves; the ref stays.

props.segment: Stable Reference IDs Survive Viewport Chaos.

beats.narration_text: Watch the viewport shrink. The pixel coordinates under the button change — 960, 540 becomes 720, 405. But the ref label, 'confirm underscore order underscore 1', stays glued to the button through the entire resize. Claude always targets the ref, and it always hits.

props.segment: Stable Reference IDs Survive Viewport Chaos.
props.command:
Before: (960, 540) — After resize: (720, 405)
ref="confirm_order_1" — unchanged in both states

viz.description: Two-state animation on cream stage. State 1: browser frame at 1920×1080, button centered, terracotta label 'ref=confirm_order_1' glued to button, coordinate chip '(960, 540)' in INK_SOFT beneath. Arrow shows morph transition. State 2: frame shrinks to 1440×900, button moves, coordinate chip changes to '(720, 405)' in INK_SOFT — but the terracotta ref label stays attached to the button at all times. Final frame: 'Claude clicks ref=confirm_order_1. Lands every time.' Source: Claude Quickstarts (Anthropic).

beats.act: honesty beat — what this does NOT cover
beats.narration_text: What this does not cover: the JavaScript execution details — how the browser element script assigns IDs, or CSS specificity rules that affect ref injection. And it does not cover dynamic elements that appear after page load. Those require a re-injection pass. This video is the ref mechanism, not the full tool harness.

props.segment: Stable Reference IDs Survive Viewport Chaos.
props.command: Exclusions: JS injection details, CSS specificity, dynamic post-load elements (need re-injection). This video: the ref mechanism only.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Pixel coordinates are coordinates in one viewport at one moment. Resize the window and they expire. Stable element refs survive because they attach to the element, not its position. JavaScript assigns them; Claude targets them by name. The automation stops caring about pixels.

artifactLines: Pixel coordinates tie a click to one viewport at one moment — resize the window and they expire.
JavaScript assigns stable ref IDs (e.g., ref='confirm_order_1') to every clickable element before Claude sees the page.
The ref stays glued to the element through resize and reflow; the pixel coordinates change beneath it.
Claude targets the ref, not the pixels. The automation never breaks on resize.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. The prompt: 'Assign stable refs to every clickable element on this page so my automation survives a resize.' That prompt asks Claude to write the injection script. What ref-naming scheme does it choose? Does it handle iframes? Does it guard against duplicate IDs? Run it and see. Liam, in for Bear.

props.segment: Stable Reference IDs Survive Viewport Chaos.
props.command: Assign stable refs to every clickable element on this page so my automation survives a resize.
